title: Ixeris yoshinoi
taxonomy: Ixeris yoshinoi is classified as a species of flowering plant in the dandelion tribe within the daisy family, Asteraceae. It belongs to the genus Ixeris. The classification of Ixeris yoshinoi further places it in the tribe Cichorieae and the family Asteraceae. Within the genus Ixeris, it is known to be a species native to East Asia. As a member of the family Asteraceae, it shares characteristics with other daisy family members.

intro: Ixeris yoshinoi, also known as Yoshino's dandelion, is a perennial plant species belonging to the Asteraceae family. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including digestive issues, fever, and inflammation, due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Ixeris yoshinoi include flavonoids, sesquiterpenes, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. In traditional herbal medicine, Ixeris yoshinoi is commonly used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat digestive problems, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections.
